member.name: Lee Pitcher
member.constituency: Doncaster East and the Isle of Axholme
member.role: MP for Doncaster East and the Isle of Axholme

currentMembership.constituency: Doncaster East and the Isle of Axholme

addresses: Armstrong House, First Avenue, Finningley, DN9 3GA

member.synopsis: Lee Pitcher is the Labour MP for Doncaster East and the Isle of Axholme , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Migraines: Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to encourage employers to provide reasonable workplace support for employees affected by migraine.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to working collaboratively to support disabled people and people with health conditions, including people affected by migraine. The Department of Health and Social Care and the Department for Work and Pensions’ Joint Work and H...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Migraines: Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent discussions he has had with the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care on cross-government support for people whose ability to work is affected by migraine.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to working collaboratively to support disabled people and people with health conditions, including people affected by migraine. The Department of Health and Social Care and the Department for Work and Pensions’ Joint Work and H...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Migraines: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to help improve outcomes for people living with migraine.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the significant impact that migraine can have on individuals’ quality of life and is committed to improving care and outcomes for people living with this condition.NHS England is supporting improvements in migraine care through n...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
